recommendations.title: Ek Tha Tiger
recommendations.overview: A top Indian agent is sent to observe the actions of a scientist but the mission complicates when he falls in love with the scientist's Pakistani caretaker.

recommendations.title: Tiger Zinda Hai
recommendations.overview: When a group of Indian and Pakistani nurses are held hostage in Iraq by a terrorist organization, a renowned Indian secret agent is drawn out of hiding to rescue them.

recommendations.title: My Beautiful Laundrette
recommendations.overview: A young Pakistani Briton manages a rundown laundrette with his lover while dealing with tension in his family, the local Pakistani community, and a persistent mob of skinheads.

recommendations.title: Drone
recommendations.overview: Ideologies collide with fatal results when a military drone contractor meets an enigmatic Pakistani businessman.

recommendations.title: The Courier
recommendations.overview: Cold War spy Greville Wynne and his Russian source try to put an end to the Cuban Missile Crisis.

recommendations.title: The Round Up
recommendations.overview: A faithful retelling of the 1942 "Vel' d'Hiv Roundup" and the events surrounding it.

recommendations.title: I'm Still Here
recommendations.overview: A woman married to a former politician during the 1971 military dictatorship in Brazil is forced to reinvent herself and chart a new course for her family after a violent and arbitrary act.

recommendations.title: The Reluctant Fundamentalist
recommendations.overview: In New York, a Pakistani native finds that his American Dream has collapsed in the wake of the 9/11 terrorist attacks.

recommendations.title: A Wedding
recommendations.overview: Zahira, 18, is close to her family until her parents ask her to follow Pakistani tradition to choose a husband. Torn between family customs and her western lifestyle, the young woman turns for help to her brother and confidant Amir.

recommendations.title: Airlift
recommendations.overview: When Iraq invades Kuwait in August, 1990, a callous Indian businessman becomes the spokesperson for more than 170,000 stranded countrymen.
